decisions.reason: Completes the map sentence: 'The light green are the Sunni people...'.
Completes the definition that begins 'So Muslim means'.
Completes Muhammad's mission as messenger of God and revelation to pagan Arabia.
Completes the geographic phrase 'all across the Middle East'.
Joining 'The spread of' to 'the Al-Aqsaq Mosque is very interesting' creates an incoherent splice rather than an obvious sentence completion.
Completes 'They take their religion very, very seriously.'
Completes the clause explaining what rebuilding the Temple would require.
Completes 'You can get on a plane...'.
Completes 'And this is where the Abbasid Caliphate was.'
Completes 'it is the Abbasids who would initiate...'.
Completes the sentence about taking knowledge inside and outside the Islamic world.
Completes 'Both algebra and algorithm were originally Arabic words.'
Completes the statement that the school is the first degree-granting university.
Completes the definition of asabiyah as egalitarian, free, and cohesive.
Completes the clause that Jews and Christians knew how to read and write.
Completes the phrase 'entire Middle East'.
Completes the phrase identifying Heraclius as the emperor.
Moves the list starter 'Abraham was the first' to the following list sequence.
Completes the interpretation that some Jews could read Muhammad as the Messiah figure.
Completes 'the best evidence for my argument is actually the Quran.'
Completes the quoted Quranic question.
Completes 'what Muhammad is saying...'.
Completes Muhammad confirming what people believed about God.
Completes the claim about Islam seeing monotheism's simplicity and absoluteness.
Completes the phrase 'fulfillment of the law and prophets'.
Completes 'first of all Islam marks a revolution.'
Completes 'It's not a conflict. It's not a conquest.'
Moves the conditional starter 'Because if' to its following clause.
Completes the comparison to what Cyrus the Great did.
Completes the controversial claim that it was originally meant to be the third temple.
Completes 'respect for learning and for literacy.'
Completes 'the third major problem in the tradition is faith versus history.'
Completes the sentence about Jesus' sacrifice establishing ultimate good.
Completes 'A lot of the ideas in Christianity...'.
Completes the claim that God can come through faith, devotion, and practice.
Completes the boundary even though the ASR wording is awkward; it belongs to the prior Bible-contradiction sentence.
Completes the sentence about innovations becoming dogma over time.
Completes the sequence of empirical observation, analysis, and belief.
Completes the definition of eternal as always having been and always being there.
Completes Plato's ascent through mathematics and geometry.
Completes 'And it's called telos.'
Moves 'And they will' to the following phrase 'do so through three major events'.
Completes the culminating claim that the Islamic Golden Age is proto-modernity.
Completes the question 'When was Shakespeare writing?'.
Completes the preview sentence about China being brought into world history.
